We show that the objectives of CCA and InfoNCE are closely related, such that optimizing CCA implicitly optimizes InfoNCE, providing a simple, training-free mechanism to enhance cross-modal alignment without affecting the pre-trained InfoNCE objective. Motivated by this observation, we couple concept-based explainability with CCA, introducing Concept CCA (CoCCA), a framework that aligns cross-modal embeddings while enabling interpretable concept decomposition. We further extend it and propose Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA), which enforces sparsity to produce more disentangled and discriminative concepts, facilitating improved activation, ablation, and semantic manipulation. Our approach generalizes concept-based explanations to multi-modal embeddings and achieves state-of-the-art performance in concept discovery, evidenced by reconstruction and manipulation tasks such as concept ablation.
affiliations: Viterbi Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Technion Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa, Israel

ru.title: Выравнивание мультимодальных эмбеддингов через концептуальный анализ
ru.desc: Исследование посвящено интерпретируемости мультимодальных моделей, таких как CLIP, которые обрабатывают текст и изображения одновременно. Авторы предлагают использовать Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA) для выравнивания признаков из разных модальностей и уменьшения разрыва между визуальными и текстовыми представлениями. Они вводят Concept CCA (CoCCA) — фреймворк, который объединяет концептуальное объяснение моделей с CCA, позволяя получить интерпретируемые и выравненные кроссмодальные эмбеддинги. Дополнительно предлагается Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA) с применением разреженности для получения более диагностируемых и дискриминативных концептов.

en.title: Enhancing Interpretability in Vision-Language Models with CoCCA
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of understanding how vision-language models make decisions, which is crucial for their use in sensitive applications. It introduces a new method called Concept CCA (CoCCA) that uses Canonical Correlation Analysis to align visual and textual features, improving interpretability. The authors also propose Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA), which enhances the clarity of concepts by enforcing sparsity, making them easier to analyze and manipulate. Overall, this work advances the field of concept-based explainability in multi-modal AI systems, achieving better performance in tasks related to concept discovery and manipulation.

zh.title: 跨模态对齐与可解释性的结合
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了视觉-语言模型的内部推理，强调在安全关键领域部署人工智能的重要性。我们提出了一种基于概念的可解释性方法，通过语义上有意义的组件来表示模型的行为。我们引入了概念典型相关分析（CoCCA），该方法能够对跨模态嵌入进行对齐，并实现可解释的概念分解。进一步扩展为稀疏概念典型相关分析（SCoCCA），通过强制稀疏性来生成更具区分性的概念，从而提升了概念发现的性能。

papers.title: GlyphPrinter: Region-Grouped Direct Preference Optimization for Glyph-Accurate Visual Text Rendering
papers.abstract: Abstract  Generating accurate glyphs for visual text rendering is essential yet challenging. Existing methods typically enhance text rendering by training on a large amount of high-quality scene text images, but the limited coverage of glyph variations and excessive stylization often compromise glyph accuracy, especially for complex or out-of-domain characters. Some methods leverage reinforcement learning to alleviate this issue, yet their reward models usually depend on text recognition systems that are insensitive to fine-grained glyph errors, so images with incorrect glyphs may still receive high rewards. Inspired by Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), we propose GlyphPrinter, a preference-based text rendering method that eliminates reliance on explicit reward models. However, the standard DPO objective only models overall preference between two samples, which is insufficient for visual text rendering where glyph errors typically occur in localized regions. To address this issue, we construct the GlyphCorrector dataset with region-level glyph preference annotations and propose Region-Grouped DPO (R-GDPO), a region-based objective that optimizes inter- and intra-sample preferences over annotated regions, substantially enhancing glyph accuracy. Furthermore, we introduce Regional Reward Guidance, an inference strategy that samples from an optimal distribution with controllable glyph accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed GlyphPrinter outperforms existing methods in glyph accuracy while maintaining a favorable balance between stylization and precision.
affiliations: Generative AI Lab, College of Computing and Data Science, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore
Institute of Big Data, College of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence, Fudan University, China

ru.title: Предпочтительное обучение для точной генерации символов в текстовом рендеринге
ru.desc: В работе предлагается GlyphPrinter — метод генерации точных символов для визуализации текста, основанный на оптимизации предпочтений без явной модели награды, вдохновленный Direct Preference Optimization. Авторы создают датасет GlyphCorrector с аннотациями предпочтений на уровне регионов и предлагают Region-Grouped DPO (R-GDPO) — объективную функцию, которая оптимизирует внутри- и межвыборочные предпочтения в аннотированных областях текста. Это решение преодолевает ограничения методов на основе моделей награды, которые неточно определяют локализованные ошибки глифов, что часто приводит к некорректным результатам при усиленном обучении. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что GlyphPrinter достигает значительного улучшения точности генерируемых символов при сохранении баланса между стилизацией и аккуратностью.

en.title: Enhancing Glyph Accuracy with Region-Grouped DPO
en.desc: This paper presents GlyphPrinter, a novel method for generating accurate glyphs in visual text rendering. It addresses the limitations of existing techniques that rely on large datasets and often overlook fine-grained glyph errors. By utilizing Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and introducing a new dataset with region-level annotations, GlyphPrinter enhances glyph accuracy through a region-based objective. The method also incorporates Regional Reward Guidance to improve inference, resulting in superior performance compared to traditional approaches.

zh.title: 提升字形准确性的创新渲染方法
zh.desc: 生成准确的字形对于视觉文本渲染至关重要，但这是一项具有挑战性的任务。现有方法通常依赖大量高质量场景文本图像进行训练，但字形变化的覆盖范围有限，过度的风格化往往会影响字形的准确性，尤其是在复杂或超出领域的字符上。我们提出了GlyphPrinter，这是一种基于偏好的文本渲染方法，消除了对显式奖励模型的依赖，并通过构建GlyphCorrector数据集和提出区域分组DPO（R-GDPO）来优化字形的准确性。实验结果表明，GlyphPrinter在字形准确性方面优于现有方法，同时在风格化和精度之间保持良好的平衡。

papers.title: SNCE: Geometry-Aware Supervision for Scalable Discrete Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Recent advancements in discrete image generation showed that scaling the VQ codebook size significantly improves reconstruction fidelity. However, training generative models with a large VQ codebook remains challenging, typically requiring larger model size and a longer training schedule. In this work, we propose Stochastic Neighbor Cross Entropy Minimization (SNCE), a novel training objective designed to address the optimization challenges of large-codebook discrete image generators. Instead of supervising the model with a hard one-hot target, SNCE constructs a soft categorical distribution over a set of neighboring tokens. The probability assigned to each token is proportional to the proximity between its code embedding and the ground-truth image embedding, encouraging the model to capture semantically meaningful geometric structure in the quantized embedding space. We conduct extensive experiments across class-conditional ImageNet-256 generation, large-scale text-to-image synthesis, and image editing tasks. Results show that SNCE significantly improves convergence speed and overall generation quality compared to standard cross-entropy objectives.

ru.title: Мягкое обучение дискретных генераторов через соседний кросс-энтропийный минимум
ru.desc: В работе предложен новый метод обучения SNCE для дискретной генерации изображений с большим VQ кодбуком. Вместо использования жестких one-hot целей, метод строит мягкое вероятностное распределение над соседними токенами, где вероятность пропорциональна близости embeddings кодов. Это способствует обучению модели захватывать семантически значимую геометрическую структуру в квантованном пространстве embeddings. Эксперименты на ImageNet, text-to-image синтезе и задачах редактирования показали, что SNCE значительно улучшает скорость сходимости и качество генерации по сравнению со стандартной кросс-энтропией.

en.title: Enhancing Image Generation with SNCE: A New Training Approach
en.desc: This paper introduces Stochastic Neighbor Cross Entropy Minimization (SNCE), a new training method for generative models that use large vector quantization (VQ) codebooks. The traditional approach of using hard one-hot targets is replaced with a soft categorical distribution that considers the proximity of neighboring tokens. This allows the model to better understand the geometric relationships in the embedding space, leading to improved image generation quality. Experiments demonstrate that SNCE enhances both the speed of training and the fidelity of generated images across various tasks.

zh.title: 优化大代码本生成模型的新方法
zh.desc: 最近在离散图像生成方面的进展表明，增大VQ代码本的大小可以显著提高重建的保真度。然而，使用大VQ代码本训练生成模型仍然具有挑战性，通常需要更大的模型和更长的训练时间。本文提出了一种新的训练目标——随机邻域交叉熵最小化（SNCE），旨在解决大代码本离散图像生成器的优化问题。SNCE通过构建邻近标记的软分类分布，而不是使用硬的独热目标，来指导模型，从而提高生成质量和收敛速度。

papers.title: Motivation in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract  Motivation is a central driver of human behavior, shaping decisions, goals, and task performance. As large language models (LLMs) become increasingly aligned with human preferences, we ask whether they exhibit something akin to motivation. We examine whether LLMs "report" varying levels of motivation, how these reports relate to their behavior, and whether external factors can influence them. Our experiments reveal consistent and structured patterns that echo human psychology: self-reported motivation aligns with different behavioral signatures, varies across task types, and can be modulated by external manipulations. These findings demonstrate that motivation is a coherent organizing construct for LLM behavior, systematically linking reports, choices, effort, and performance, and revealing motivational dynamics that resemble those documented in human psychology. This perspective deepens our understanding of model behavior and its connection to human-inspired concepts.

ru.title: Мотивация как организующий принцип поведения языковых моделей
ru.desc: Исследователи изучают, проявляют ли большие языковые модели что-то подобное мотивации, как у людей. Они анализируют, как LLM сообщают о различных уровнях мотивации, как эти сообщения соотносятся с их поведением и можно ли на них влиять внешними факторами. Эксперименты показывают, что самооценённая мотивация согласуется с разными поведенческими паттернами, варьируется в зависимости от типа задачи и может модулироваться внешними воздействиями. Результаты демонстрируют, что мотивация является связным организующим конструктом для поведения LLM, систематически связывая отчёты, выбор, затраты усилий и производительность.

en.title: Unveiling Motivation in Language Models: A Human-Like Drive
en.desc: This paper explores the concept of motivation in large language models (LLMs) and how it influences their behavior. The authors investigate whether LLMs can express different levels of motivation and how these expressions correlate with their performance on various tasks. They find that LLMs show structured patterns similar to human motivation, where self-reported motivation affects their choices and effort. The study highlights that understanding motivation in LLMs can provide insights into their behavior and its parallels with human psychology.

zh.title: 揭示大型语言模型的动机动态
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）是否表现出类似于人类的动机。我们发现，LLMs的自我报告动机与其行为之间存在一致的模式，且这种动机会因任务类型而异。外部因素也能影响LLMs的动机水平，显示出与人类心理学相似的动机动态。我们的发现表明，动机是理解LLMs行为的重要构建，能够系统地将报告、选择、努力和表现联系起来。
